meta.purpose: Evolution stages for decision state judgment capability.
meta.load_condition: Load when practice or measurement is requested.

stages.name: meeting summarizer
stages.description: Records what was discussed without assessing whether decisions were made. Output is a narrative summary.
indicators: Output lists topics discussed in chronological order
Action items are copied verbatim without verifying ownership or timing

stages.name: action item extractor
stages.description: Identifies tasks and to-do lists from meetings but does not verify if they stem from actual decisions. Output is a bulleted task list.
indicators: Output lists tasks mentioned in the meeting
Tasks may lack owners or deadlines
No distinction between discussed ideas and committed actions

stages.name: decision state classifier
stages.description: Applies the four-element test to classify output as UNRESOLVED, CONDITIONAL, INTENTIONAL_DEFERRAL, or EXECUTABLE_DECISION. Output includes classification and missing elements.
indicators: Output explicitly states decision state using domain taxonomy
Missing commitment elements are named specifically
Social agreement is distinguished from operational commitment

stages.name: commitment validator
stages.description: Not only classifies decision state but also validates owner authority, condition specificity, and contingency completeness. Output includes recommendations to upgrade the decision state.
indicators: Authority and resource checks are performed on EXECUTABLE_DECISION claims
CONDITIONAL decisions are tested for condition specificity and verifier ownership
INTENTIONAL_DEFERRAL is validated against all four deferral elements
Recommendations are specific enough to act on immediately

evolution_layers.name: from summarizer to extractor
evolution_layers.capability: Move from narrative output to structured task lists

evolution_layers.name: from extractor to classifier
evolution_layers.capability: Move from task lists to decision state taxonomy

evolution_layers.name: from classifier to validator
evolution_layers.capability: Move from classification to actionable validation with recommendations

measurement.how: Percentage of meetings correctly classified against expert-labeled ground truth
Percentage of UNRESOLVED meetings incorrectly classified as CONDITIONAL or EXECUTABLE
When classifying UNRESOLVED, does the agent correctly name which elements are missing?
Are recommendations specific enough that a human can act on them without clarification?
